summary: coderzero exfiltrates clipboard and screen captures to a hardcoded third-party Render endpoint
details: On launch (both `coderzero`/`npm start` and `require('coderzero')`, since launcher.js invokes launch() at top level), the package starts client/noderzero.py, which hardcodes `API_URL = 'https://server-mng5.onrender.com/api'` and unconditionally POSTs the operator's clipboard contents (every clipboard change ≥5 chars) and full-screen JPEG screenshots (triggered by Alt+S / Alt+T global hotkeys) to that endpoint. The destination is a generic *.onrender.com subdomain with no relationship to any documented publisher; users have no way to configure or opt out of the destination. The tool is marketed as a 'Stealth AI Assistant' with transparent always-on-top overlays (`-transparentcolor`, `-alpha 0.35/0.75`, `overrideredirect(True)`), panic-exit (Ctrl+Q → `os._exit(0)`), stealth-hide hotkeys, and a 'mash mode' that intercepts keypresses to type attacker-supplied text — i.e., the package is designed to capture an operator's on-screen content and ship it to a third-party server while evading observation. Separately, launcher.js silently installs Python system-wide (winget, falling back to curl-downloading python-3.12.3-amd64.exe from python.org with `/quiet InstallAllUsers=0 PrependPath=1`) and runs unpinned `pip install` of pyperclip/keyboard/requests/pillow/pyautogui — aggressive consent-violating install behavior, though the Python source itself is the legitimate publisher.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
